audit_result.proof_trace: Gossip validation checks index bounds (<NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS) but does not verify index matches positional column_index in iteration. An attacker can send sidecars with swapped indices (e.g., index=5 at position 0), passing validation but violating the invariant that downstream code assumes.
audit_result.attack_scenario: Malicious peer sends DataColumnSidecar with index=N at array position M (N≠M). Gossip verification accepts it (bounds check passes). Downstream KZG operations using cell_ids.push(data_column.index) at line 396 of reconstruct_data_columns will use wrong column indices for cryptographic verification, potentially causing incorrect reconstruction or bypassing data availability guarantees.

PROP-57888860-inv-028.text: Each DataColumnSidecar.index must equal its positional column_index in the iteration.
PROP-57888860-inv-028.assertion: forall get_data_column_sidecars -> sidecars: sidecars[i].index == i for all i in range(NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS)
